description: A `switch` statement NESTED inside a function body fires E-SWITCH-FORBIDDEN. Pins the nested-logic-body statement-loop fire site — a guard distinct from the top-level one (`ctrl-switch-forbidden-logic-block-pos`); the ban does not weaken with nesting depth.
rationale: §34 (E-SWITCH-FORBIDDEN, cross-ref §17) states the ban on the KEYWORD unconditionally — "the JavaScript `switch` keyword is not part of scrml's vocabulary" — with no locus carve-out. A vocabulary exclusion is not depth-scoped: a `switch` inside a function body is as much not-scrml as one at logic-block top level, so the code SHALL fire here too. This case is the direct mirror of `ctrl-switch-forbidden-match-neg`, which writes the same toggle with the sanctioned `match` form.
